ribba_size_data.note: Use this exact data in the main size chart table. All pixel values are at 300 DPI. Portrait sizes listed — landscape is dimensions swapped.

sizes.notes: Great for small gallery walls, bedside frames
Most popular Ribba size. 4:5 ratio = same family as 16×20
Widely used in European markets. 3:4 ratio.
Statement wall size. Same ratio family as 8×10

sizes.snaptosize_pack: Not in standard packs — create separately at 6000×8400 px
sizes.notes: Poster format. 5:7 ratio — not 2:3. Closest SnapToSize size is 20×30 (2:3). Sellers must create a dedicated file or crop a small strip from a 20×30.

key_insights_for_writer: The core pain: Ribba frames use metric sizing (cm) internally, sold in inch equivalents in the US. This confuses sellers who look up '30x40cm' and get different pixel numbers than '12x16 inch' — they're the same frame.
The SnapToSize win: 8×10, 12×16, and 16×20 are all covered by existing packs (4:5 and 3:4). One upload → all three automatically. Only 5×7 (Extras) and 20×28 (nonstandard) need special attention.

The 20×28 nuance: This is a 5:7 ratio, NOT a standard 2:3 (which would be 20×30). Sellers who upload a 2:3 file and assume it fits 20×28 will get a slightly cropped result. The page should explain this clearly — it's a genuine pain point nobody addresses.

Portrait/landscape is easy: SnapToSize generates both orientations in every pack. If your design is portrait-only, you still get landscape files in the ZIP — just note buyers will need to select the right one.
Pixel math must be clearly shown: this is a reference page. Include the table prominently, above the fold if possible. Sellers come here for the numbers — earn trust with accuracy, then convert.

Competitor gap: custommat.ca and printforfun.sg list matboard dimensions (the opening behind the frame mat), NOT the print size. This creates confusion — a seller following custommat.ca guidance will create a print too small. Our page should address this: use the full frame size, not the mat opening, for digital prints.
keywords.competitor_gaps: No page currently answers 'what pixel dimensions do I create for IKEA Ribba frames at 300 DPI' from an Etsy seller perspective. IKEA.com lists product specs only. custommat.ca and printforfun.sg address physical matboard cutting for print shops — not digital file creation for Etsy. Reddit threads are confused and contradictory on the 12x16 vs 30x40cm equivalency. YouTube has no dedicated guide. This is a fully open niche.

competitor_approaches.approach: Product catalog only — lists frame dimensions, colors, materials. Zero guidance on what print size to create.
Physical matboard cutting guide for print shops. Lists mat opening sizes (smaller than frame), not digital print sizes. Will mislead digital sellers.
Singapore print shop guide. Focuses on physical photo prints, cm-only sizing, no pixel dimensions or DPI guidance. No Etsy angle.

search_signals.volume_signal: SERP for 'IKEA Ribba frame sizes' has IKEA.com + two small print shop blogs + Reddit. No tool-focused or Etsy-seller-focused page. Low competition, high buyer intent — the searcher is actively trying to create or buy a print. Ribba is IKEA's flagship frame series, globally recognized. Traffic ceiling is solid for a niche reference page.

keywords.conversion_angle: Reference page with table-first layout. Earn trust with accurate pixel data upfront (this is what they came for). Mid-page: reveal that 3 of the 5 Ribba sizes are already in SnapToSize packs — one upload handles 8×10, 12×16, 16×20 automatically. CTA: 'Try Free — Upload once, get all Ribba sizes ready to sell.' Final CTA after FAQ. UTM: ?source=seo_ikea-ribba-frame-print-sizes&kind=guide
keywords.page_design_notes: Clean, light background (not dark — this is a reference/info page, not a concept/problem page). Hero: bold headline + the key stat ('3 of 5 Ribba sizes covered automatically'). Main table should be visually distinct — use teal accent (#2DD4BF) to highlight the rows where SnapToSize packs auto-cover the size. Add a small SnapToSize badge/chip on those rows. After table: mid-page ContextualCTA. Then explain the 20×28 nuance section. Then FAQ. Then FinalCTA. Include ListingOutputShowcase component. Include RelatedPages component at the bottom pointing to: etsy-print-sizes, etsy-8x10-print-size, print-size-for-frame-guide, etsy-print-ratios.

Also add from etsy-8x10-print-size: '8×10 is the most popular IKEA Ribba frame size — perfect 4:5 ratio.' And from etsy-print-sizes pillar: mention IKEA Ribba as a common frame standard with link.
